scenes.dramaturgical_function: Initiates the Debate beat; the fear of societal and familial rejection crashes down on him.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel drops to his knees and digs a shallow grave in the sand. He hovers the guitar over the hole, his hands shaking as he prepares to bury it. He squeezes his eyes shut, but at the last second, he yanks the guitar to his chest, hugging it tightly. He cannot let it go.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Concludes the Debate beat; Axel makes the internal choice to keep the instrument, setting up the public confrontation.

scenes.narrative_action: Coral aggressively polishes Axel's coral-flute with a kelp cloth, delivering a tight-lipped, passive-aggressive lecture about how 'we must maintain perfect tempo today.' Axel nods rapidly, aggressively complimenting her polishing technique to deflect, while using his tail to subtly kick a kelp-wrapped bundle behind a prop rock.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Re-establishes the suffocating familial stakes right before the climax.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel steps out onto the massive, perfectly symmetrical stage. Hundreds of synchronized citizens sit in dead silence. On the podium, Maestro Barnaby glares down, hums a perfect 'A' note to center himself, and raises his baton. Axel grips his polished flute, sweating profusely.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates the tension by placing Axel in the ultimate crucible of conformity.

scenes.narrative_action: Barnaby sharply flicks his baton to cue the start. Axel raises the flute to his lips but freezes. His foot begins to tap a frantic, off-beat rhythm against the stage. Barnaby's eye twitches violently. The silence stretches, heavy and suffocating.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Pushes Axel to the breaking point where his internal nature physically rejects the external expectation.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel lowers the flute. He opens his hand and lets the delicate instrument drop to the hard stage floor. It shatters into dozens of pieces. The audience gasps in unison. In the front row, Coral grips her cello bow, her knuckles turning white.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The point of no return; Axel publicly destroys his tie to the classical world.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel darts behind the prop rock and rips the kelp wrapping off the bright red electric guitar. He steps back onto the center stage, strapping the heavy instrument across his chest. Barnaby lowers his baton, his mouth falling open in horror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The reveal of the forbidden tool to the establishment.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel winds up his arm and strikes the strings with everything he has. A blistering, distorted power chord blasts through the water, literally shattering a decorative glass-sponge on the stage. As the judges recoil, Axel shoots them a manic grin, turns his back, and swims rapidly out of the amphitheater.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Break into Two climax; Axel liberates himself and officially begins his punk rock rebellion.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel drags his heavy red electric guitar through the murky, algae-covered outskirts of town. He spots Puff, an oversized pufferfish, wedged miserably beneath a collapsed archway of dead coral. Puff is hyperventilating, his body inflating and deflating in erratic bursts that kick up embarrassing clouds of silt into the water.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the B-Story and establishes Puff's core wound of taking up too much space and ruining the peace.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel approaches. Puff shrinks away, literally holding his breath to avoid speaking, his cheeks bulging. Axel doesn't shush him; instead, he taps a syncopated, irregular rhythm on his guitar body. He hands Puff two heavy driftwood sticks and points to a cluster of hollow clam shells. Puff hesitates, trembling, then brings a stick down with a clumsy thwack. Axel cheers. Puff hits harder, his nervous hyperventilation transforming into a steady, thunderous drum beat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Axel acts as a mentor, proving to Puff that his massive size and impact are powerful tools, not shameful flaws.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel and Puff float outside a sterile holding pen. Inside, Pip, a heavily scarred mantis shrimp, paces at blinding speed. Her spring-loaded claws twitch aggressively. She stops pacing only to aggressively shadow-box the water, clearly bored and brimming with unspent, destructive energy.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces Pip and her stifled need for destruction, setting her up as the catalyst for the band's chaotic energy.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel slides a thick, four-stringed bass guitar—cobbled from sunken ship debris—through the cell bars. Pip doesn't ask questions. She snatches the instrument, winds up, and plucks a string with a concussive snap. The shockwave shatters the heavy lock on her gate. She swims out, offering Axel an aggressively firm high-five.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Completes the band's lineup by weaponizing Pip's destructive nature into musical rebellion.

scenes.narrative_action: The trio sets up their gear among Coral's pristine, perfectly aligned gardening tools and spare cello wax. With the door shut, they start to play. Puff thrashes wildly, losing the tempo in a panic. Pip attacks her bass, sending shockwaves that knock the tools off the walls. Axel screams over the deafening, uncoordinated wall of noise. Amidst the complete wreckage of the garage, the three share a look of pure, unadulterated joy.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The first threshold of the Fun and Games; tests the band in a confined, domestic space to highlight their messy dynamic.

scenes.narrative_action: The abrasive racket bleeds through the garage walls. Outside, synchronized schools of fish break their hypnotic swimming patterns, scattering in sheer panic. Furious neighbors swarm the driveway, aggressively tapping their fins against the garage door in demanding unison. In the distance, the wailing siren of Maestro Barnaby's barracuda guards echoes down the street.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The establishment pushes back; delivers immediate, physical consequences for breaking Aqua-Ville's strict conformity.

scenes.narrative_action: Hearing the sirens, Axel, Puff, and Pip scramble in a panic. They shove their heavy instruments into bags. Axel kicks open a rusted back vent. Puff squeezes his massive frame through just as the front garage door bursts open, revealing a terrifyingly precise choir of baton-wielding barracudas. The band vanishes into the murky drainage current.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Forces the band to flee the safety of the town, pushing them past the point of no return.

scenes.narrative_action: The band descends into a jagged, lightless chasm swirling with unpredictable thermal vents. The town's metronomic hum fades away completely. Surrounded by towering black basalt and glowing algae, Axel plugs his red guitar into a jury-rigged amplifier powered by a writhing cluster of electric eels.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes a new, dangerous, but liberating sanctuary where the band can operate without limits.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel barks a frantic, four-beat count. They play. This time, Puff's heavy drums lock perfectly with Pip's concussive bass thrum. Axel's fingers fly across the fretboard, unleashing a blistering riff. They forge a massive, physical wall of sound—a cohesive punk anthem. Axel, Puff, and Pip scream out the final chorus together, basking in the euphoric rush of their unified, chaotic identity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The emotional high point of the sequence; the misfits finally connect, mastering their flaws to find true belonging.

scenes.narrative_action: Maestro Barnaby sits alone in his perfect sanctuary, his eyes closed, humming a flawless 'A' note. Suddenly, the water ripples. Heavy, distorted bass frequencies travel up from the trench and slam into the amphitheater. The water in Barnaby's delicate crystal goblet vibrates violently, then shatters into dust. Barnaby's eyes snap open in horror. He grips his conductor's baton until his knuckles turn white, his eye twitching furiously.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Alerts the antagonist to the rebellion, turning the band's triumph into a looming, high-stakes threat.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel, Puff, and Pip are violently spat out of a rusted drainage pipe into the murky, unregulated waters outside the city limits. Puff inflates slightly in panic, wedging his massive body in the pipe opening. Pip aggressively shadow-boxes the metal grate, punching Puff loose into the open water. Axel catches his heavy red guitar, looks back at the pristine, glowing dome of Aqua-Ville, and points his fretboard toward the dark, looming abyss in the distance.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions the band from the threshold of the city into the dangerous unknown, establishing their commitment to fleeing the establishment.

scenes.narrative_action: The trio navigates a graveyard of bleached, silent coral. The comforting metronomic hum of Aqua-Ville fades into dead silence. Puff begins hyperventilating, his cheeks bulging as he literally holds his breath to avoid making noise. Axel swims up beside him, gently tapping a syncopated, irregular rhythm on his guitar body to ground the anxious pufferfish, physically reminding him of their new, shared pulse.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Builds tension as they approach the trench, testing Puff's anxiety and solidifying Axel's role as the band's empathetic leader.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. THE FORBIDDEN TRENCH - DESCENT - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: They dive over the edge of a jagged, lightless chasm. The water instantly turns cold and heavy. A sudden, violent thermal vent blasts them upward, spinning Axel out of control. Pip laughs maniacally, extending her spring-loaded claws to grapple onto the towering black basalt walls. She physically pulls Axel and Puff down through the turbulent, unpredictable currents.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the physical danger and chaotic nature of the trench, forcing the band to rely on Pip's destructive strength to survive the descent.

scenes.narrative_action: They touch down on the trench floor, illuminated only by patches of eerie, glowing bioluminescent algae. Axel unpacks his guitar and spots a writhing cluster of glowing electric eels nestled in a rock fissure. He carefully wrangles the snapping eels, plugging a jury-rigged kelp-cable into the cluster to power his makeshift amplifier.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sets the stage for the midpoint performance, showcasing Axel's resourcefulness and the trench's raw, untamed energy.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel plugs the kelp-cable into his guitar. The red finish glows ominously with a crackling BZZZT. Puff fumbles his heavy driftwood sticks, dropping them into the silt and apologizing profusely. Pip impatiently tunes her bass, snapping the strings aggressively to drown out his apologies. Axel stops them. He deflects the tension by aggressively complimenting Pip's raw power and Puff's massive size, ordering them to stop trying to be quiet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Addresses the characters' internal flaws (Puff's anxiety, Pip's impatience) right before the ultimate test of their musical synergy.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel barks a frantic, four-beat count. Puff slams his sticks onto the hollow clam shells with massive, unrefined force. Pip plucks her thick bass string, sending a concussive shockwave through the water. Axel's fingers fly across the fretboard, unleashing a blistering, distorted riff. It starts sloppy, but they quickly lock into a fast, driving punk groove.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The catalyst of the midpoint victory; the band finally connects, weaponizing their chaotic individual traits into a cohesive, powerful force.

scenes.narrative_action: The music escalates into a raw, roaring punk anthem. The sheer physical force of their sound waves blasts away centuries of dead silt from the basalt walls, revealing vibrant, ancient neon coral underneath. Axel, Puff, and Pip scream out the final chorus together, thrashing their heads in unison. They share a look of pure, euphoric belonging amidst the noise.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The emotional high point (False Victory) of the sequence, visually proving that their 'noise' awakens life rather than destroying it.

scenes.narrative_action: The final, heavily distorted chord rings out, echoing endlessly. The massive, unchecked sound waves, visualized as rippling, distorted water, hit the swirling thermal vents above them. Instead of dissipating, the trench's unique geology acts as a massive acoustic megaphone, grabbing the heavy bass frequencies and funneling them violently upward.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Turns the band's triumph into a physical threat, initiating the sequence's turn toward impending doom as the sound escapes.

scenes.narrative_action: The heavy bass frequencies ripple through the open water like a heatwave, slamming into the pristine, perfectly manicured coral borders of Aqua-Ville. A perfectly synchronized school of classical-swimming silverfish is suddenly hit by the invisible shockwave, throwing them violently out of formation and sending them spinning in a panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates the immediate, disruptive impact of the band's unchecked music on the stifling conformity of the town.

scenes.narrative_action: Maestro Barnaby sits alone on his regal conductor's podium in absolute, sterile silence. He closes his eyes, adjusts his perfect posture, and hums a flawless 'A' note to center himself. The water around him is perfectly still, reflecting the absolute control he holds over his environment.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Re-establishes the antagonist's obsession with perfect, sterile control right before his sanctuary is violated.

scenes.narrative_action: The water in the amphitheater suddenly ripples. The chaotic, rhythmic thumping of the punk anthem invades the grand hall. Barnaby's delicate crystal goblet begins to vibrate violently on his music stand. He stares at it, paralyzed, until the glass shatters into a fine cloud of dust.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The physical manifestation of Axel's rebellion invading Barnaby's safe space, literally shattering his illusion of harmony.

scenes.narrative_action: Barnaby's eyes widen in absolute horror. His right eye twitches furiously. He grips his conductor's baton until his knuckles turn white. He does not raise his voice; instead, he coldly raises a single fin. From the dark corners of the amphitheater, a terrifyingly precise choir of barracuda guards emerges, awaiting his command.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Ends the sequence on a high-stakes cliffhanger, solidifying Barnaby's deadly retaliation against the band and ending the false victory.

scenes.narrative_action: Maestro Barnaby hovers above the square, his eyes closed, humming a flawless 'A' note. With precise, measured flicks of his baton, he directs a patrol of silver barracuda guards to aggressively correct a school of fish swimming a fraction of a beat out of sync. As the fish fall into a lethargic, hypnotic loop, a thick, gray algae visibly blooms across the pristine coral architecture, suffocating the vibrant colors. Barnaby ignores the decay, his eye only twitching when a distant, muffled vibration breaks the absolute silence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the antagonist's tyrannical control and the physical manifestation of the town's decay (the algae) caused by forced harmony.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel frantically scribbles jagged musical notes onto strips of dried kelp by the eerie glow of bioluminescent fungi. His fins are bruised and calloused. Pip impatiently snaps her heavy bass strings, shadow-boxing the water to burn off her restless energy. Puff hyperventilates, his cheeks bulging as he holds his breath, terrified of hitting his clam-shell drums too loudly. Axel stops them, his speech fast and peppered with 'bam' and 'screech' sound effects, aggressively complimenting Pip's destructive power to rally them into playing the new anthem.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Showcases the physical toll of Axel's obsession and the band's deepening synergy as they channel their flaws into the music.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel sneaks through his bedroom window, smelling of sulfurous thermal vents. Coral is already inside, white-knuckled, aggressively dusting his completely untouched classical coral-flute. She confronts him with a soothing but passive-aggressive tone, asking 'Are we sure we are getting enough sleep?' Axel stumbles over his words, deflecting by rapidly complimenting her perfectly synchronized dusting rhythm. As he backs away, a glowing, sulfur-stained piece of kelp falls from his gig bag onto the pristine floor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates maternal suspicion and highlights the fraying seams of Axel's double life through a tense, physical confrontation.

scenes.narrative_action: Coral swims through the dark, unregulated waters outside the city limits, her eyes wide with mounting panic. She grips her cello bow tightly in front of her like a weapon. She follows a distinct, glowing trail of sulfur-stained kelp debris that cuts through the graveyard of bleached coral, leading her directly toward the jagged, lightless precipice of the Forbidden Trench.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions Coral from the safety of her conformist life into the dangerous unknown, driven by maternal desperation.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. THE FORBIDDEN TRENCH - PRECIPICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Coral peers over the jagged edge of the trench. A concussive blast of distorted guitar and heavy drums hits her like a physical blow, blowing her fins back. She looks down in sheer horror at Axel, Puff, and Pip thrashing wildly to the chaotic noise. Convinced her son is engaged in deadly, self-destructive madness, she furiously waves her cello bow at a passing, perfectly synchronized barracuda patrol, pointing frantically down into the abyss.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The fatal betrayal; Coral's deeply ingrained fear of nonconformity causes her to turn her own son over to the establishment.

scenes.narrative_action: The band is mid-crescendo when a swarm of barracudas descends with brutal precision. Pip violently thrashes, snapping her claws and punching a guard in the jaw before being wrestled to the silt. Puff panics, completely inflating into a massive, helpless ball, shattering his own clam-shell kit in the process. Axel lunges desperately for his red electric guitar, but a guard violently snatches it by the neck, pinning Axel to the trench floor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The catastrophic collapse of the band, violently stripping away their sanctuary and instruments.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel is thrown roughly onto the polished coral tiles at Maestro Barnaby's feet. Barnaby looks down with icy disgust, closes his eyes, and hums a flawless 'A' note to center himself, completely ignoring Axel's frantic pleading. At Barnaby's silent nod, guards lock the red electric guitar inside a heavy, soundproof glass vault in the center of the square. In the background, Puff and Pip are forcibly hauled away into separate, high-security detention tubes.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The villain's absolute victory, physically separating the band and turning Axel's symbol of rebellion into a public monument of defeat.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel is shoved into his acoustically dampened, pastel bedroom. The heavy door locks from the outside with a resounding click. Absolute, oppressive silence fills the room. Axel slowly walks over to his desk and stares blankly at the polished coral-flute. He reaches out, trembling, but pulls his hand back as if burned. He collapses onto the edge of his perfectly made kelp bed, burying his face in his hands.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The 'All is Lost' moment; Axel is physically and psychologically trapped in the rigid mold he tried to escape.

scenes.narrative_action: Outside the window, the thick, gray algae visibly accelerates its bloom, creeping up the stalks of the family anemone and choking the vibrant polyps into a lethargic stupor. Inside, Axel curls into a tight, defeated ball on the floor, his back to the window. He completely surrenders to the crushing silence, visually mirroring the comatose state of the dying reef.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Thematic low point, directly linking Axel's internal surrender and loss of identity to the literal, physical death of the town's ecosystem.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel tumbles through his bedroom window, hastily stuffing his calloused hands into his pockets. Coral is already there, furiously polishing his classical coral-flute. She asks, 'Are we sure we are dedicating enough time to our scales?' Axel stammers out a flurry of 'bam' and 'whoosh' sound effects, aggressively complimenting her flawless polishing tempo to deflect the question. He backs into the hallway, accidentally dislodging a glowing, sulfur-stained piece of kelp from his gig bag onto the pristine floor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes the fraying of Axel's double life and provides the physical clue that will lead to his downfall.

scenes.narrative_action: Coral swims past the pristine city borders into the murky, unregulated waters. She grips her cello bow like a weapon, her knuckles completely white. She follows the glowing trail of sulfur-kelp, her eyes darting frantically at every shadow in the graveyard of bleached coral.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions Coral from her safe, conformist world into the dangerous unknown, driven by maternal fear.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel, Pip, and Puff are mid-rehearsal. Pip aggressively slaps her bass strings, shadow-boxing the water during rests. Puff's cheeks bulge as he holds his breath, sweating profusely while hammering the clam-shell drums. Axel shreds on his red electric guitar, his chaotic energy fully unleashed as he stomps on a makeshift distortion pedal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Showcases the band's peak synergy and raw emotional expression right before it is violently ripped away.

scenes.narrative_action: Coral peers over the jagged trench edge. A concussive blast of distorted punk music physically blows her fins back. Horrified by the chaotic thrashing below, she flags down a passing, perfectly synchronized barracuda patrol, pointing her cello bow frantically into the abyss.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The fatal betrayal; Coral's deeply ingrained fear of nonconformity causes her to turn her own son over to the establishment.

scenes.narrative_action: Barracudas swarm the band with brutal precision. Pip thrashes, snapping her claws and punching a guard in the jaw before being pinned to the silt. Puff panics, completely inflates into a massive ball, and shatters his own drum kit. Axel dives desperately for his guitar, but a guard violently snatches it by the neck, pressing Axel face-first into the dirt.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The catastrophic collapse of the band, violently stripping away their sanctuary and instruments.

scenes.narrative_action: Guards throw Axel onto the polished coral tiles. Maestro Barnaby hovers above him. Axel frantically pleads, his words tumbling out fast with 'screech' and 'wail' sound effects. Barnaby ignores him, closes his eyes, and hums a flawless 'A' note to center himself. Barnaby coldly dictates that Axel's dissonant tempo is a toxic threat, officially banning him from the Harmony Festival.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The antagonist's victory moment, stripping Axel of his voice and community standing.

scenes.narrative_action: At Barnaby's silent nod, guards seal Axel's red electric guitar inside a heavy, soundproof glass vault in the center of the square. Pip and Puff are dragged away in opposite directions, locked into transparent, high-security detention tubes. Axel reaches out for his bandmates, but Coral arrives and firmly yanks him back by the fin.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Physically isolates the protagonist and turns his symbol of rebellion into a public monument of defeat.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel is shoved into his pastel, acoustically dampened bedroom. Coral stands in the doorway, gripping her cello bow white-knuckled. She offers a suffocatingly soothing apology about keeping him safe before pulling the heavy door shut. The lock clicks loudly. Axel is instantly swallowed by an absolute, oppressive silence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The All is Lost moment; Axel is physically and psychologically trapped in the rigid mold he tried to escape.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel sits perfectly still on the edge of his perfectly made kelp bed. He stares at the polished coral-flute resting on his desk. He slowly reaches out, trembling, but pulls his hand back as if burned. Internalizing the town's hatred, he curls into a tight, defeated ball on the floor, completely surrendering his identity to the silence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Dark Night of the Soul; Axel internalizes the town's hatred and completely loses his identity.

scenes.narrative_action: Outside Axel's window, a thick, gray algae visibly accelerates its bloom. It creeps up the stalks of the family anemone, choking the vibrant polyps. The fish outside swim in sluggish, hypnotic loops. The camera pulls back to show the entire reef slipping into a comatose state of forced perfection, completely devoid of energy.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Thematic low point, directly linking Axel's internal surrender to the literal, physical death of the town's ecosystem.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel presses his face against the reinforced, acoustically dampened glass. Down below, the Harmony Festival creeps in synchronized, hypnotic slow-motion, blanketed by a suffocating gray algae. A massive shadow detaches from the cliff edge. The ancient Moray Eel slithers toward the helpless crowd. Axel's eyes widen. He mutters a rapid-fire string of 'whoosh' and 'screech' sound effects as the epiphany visually hits him: the town's perfect, quiet harmony is a literal death sentence.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Break into Three epiphany; Axel realizes the fatal flaw in the establishment's system and discovers his chaotic purpose.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel turns away from the window and grabs his perfectly polished classical coral-flute. Instead of playing it, he grips it like a baseball bat, swings with all his might, and violently smashes the acoustic dampening window. The heavy glass shatters outward in a cloud of bubbles. Axel darts through the jagged opening, diving headfirst into the murky water.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Crossing the threshold into action; Axel weaponizes his classical training to physically break free from his confinement.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel swims at breakneck speed, dodging lethargic, algae-dazed barracuda patrols. The guards are moving far too slowly, hypnotized by the quiet, to even raise their spears. Axel navigates the sluggish, gray maze with hyperactive agility, leaving a trail of disturbed water in his wake.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates tension and demonstrates the severe physical toll of the town's forced perfection on its defenses.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel reaches the square. In the distance, the Moray Eel is slithering closer to the stage. Axel spots his red electric guitar locked inside the heavy, soundproof glass vault. He snatches a discarded baton from a snoring guard, winds up, and shatters the vault's glass. He pulls the guitar free, the broken shards sparkling around him like a halo.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reclaiming the symbol of rebellion and arming the protagonist for the final musical battle.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. HIGH-SECURITY DETENTION ZONE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Axel bursts into the holding area. Pip is sluggishly shadow-boxing the wall of her transparent tube. Puff is huddled in the corner of his cell, holding his breath with bulging cheeks. Axel violently smashes the electronic locks with his guitar neck. Pip bursts out, aggressively high-fiving Axel, while Puff exhales in a massive, apologetic rush of bubbles.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reuniting the team and reigniting the B-story relationships just in time for the climax.

scenes.narrative_action: The trio frantically scavenges a trash pile behind the cells. Pip violently snaps rusted metal pipes in half to create makeshift bass strings. Puff nervously gathers hollowed-out sea urchin shells, hyperventilating slightly as he tests their acoustic bounce. Axel slings his guitar over his shoulder, nodding rapidly and spewing 'bam bam' noises to set their tempo.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Showcases the band's ingenuity and physically prepares them to deliver their chaotic cure.

scenes.narrative_action: The colossal Moray Eel rears up over the main stage, casting a terrifying shadow. Maestro Barnaby stands frozen at the conductor's podium. Instead of fleeing, Barnaby closes his eyes, perfectly adjusts his posture, and hums a flawless, unwavering 'A' note to center himself as the Eel unhinges its massive jaws to consume him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The antagonist's fatal flaw fully realized; Barnaby's refusal to break harmony almost gets him eaten.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel, Pip, and Puff crash onto the stage, physically knocking Barnaby out of the Eel's strike zone. Axel slams his guitar cable directly into the festival's massive acoustic amplification shells. He stomps on his makeshift distortion pedal and strikes a power chord so aggressively loud that it physically ripples the water around them.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The climax begins; the literal injection of chaotic, life-saving noise into the silent, deadly void.

scenes.narrative_action: A blistering punk anthem blasts across the square. Pip fiercely slaps the rusted pipes; Puff fully inflates, using his massive size to hammer the urchin shells with thunderous force. The concussive sound waves visibly vibrate the suffocating gray algae right off the scales of the townspeople. The sheer volume and chaotic vibrations terrify the Moray Eel, sending it thrashing wildly before it turns and flees into the dark abyss.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The musical battle that defeats the physical threat (the Eel) and shatters the thematic threat (the algae).

scenes.narrative_action: The oppressive gray algae dissolves completely into the current, revealing the brilliant, neon hues of the coral underneath. The fish wake up, gasping, and immediately begin darting and spinning to their own chaotic rhythms. Barnaby stares in shock at his ruined festival, his draconian laws completely swept away by the vibrant, messy reality unfolding before him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The resolution of the town's curse and the complete dismantling of the antagonist's control over the society.

scenes.narrative_action: Axel, Puff, and Pip launch into a joyful, fast-paced encore. In the front row, Coral watches. She grips her cello bow tightly and starts to aggressively organize some fallen kelp, but stops herself. She looks up at Axel, smiles with tears of profound pride in her eyes, and begins tapping her fins completely off-beat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Emotional resolution of the A-story (town's freedom) and B-story (mother's acceptance of Axel's true self).

scenes.narrative_action: The perspective pulls high above the reef. The once-silent waters now echo with a beautiful, raucous cacophony of sound and movement. Fish of all colors weave through the coral in a sprawling, dynamic display of individuality. Aqua-Ville is no longer a perfectly synchronized prison; it is a loud, thriving, and genuinely alive world.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Final image; cementing the thematic victory that embracing one's loud, authentic self is the ultimate key to survival.
